originalTitle: Cartoonist freed after 15 years in prison without charge in Eritrea
title: A Happy Artist is Free! Hooray!
content:
A Big Happy Welcome Back!

Hello, my super friends! Get ready for some really good news from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we have a story that will make your heart feel warm and happy. It's about a wonderful man named Biniam Solomon. He is an artist, and he has just come out of a special kind of place after a very, very long time. He is finally free, and we are all cheering for him! Biniam Solomon is from a country in Africa called Eritrea. It’s a beautiful place, and Biniam is known for drawing amazing pictures. He draws what we call "cartoons." Cartoons are drawings that can be funny, but they can also show important ideas. Biniam's cartoons made people think and sometimes even laugh out loud. He used to share his drawings in newspapers so many people could see them.

Meet Biniam the Artist!

Imagine someone who loves to draw so much that they make it their job! That’s Biniam. He is a cartoonist. A cartoonist is like a storyteller, but instead of using lots of words, they use pictures! Their drawings can show you how someone feels, what’s happening in the world, or even make a serious point in a funny way. Biniam was very good at this. His pictures were smart and made people talk about important things. Before something changed, Biniam’s cartoons were everywhere! People looked forward to seeing what new drawing he would share next. His pictures were like little puzzles that helped people understand what was going on around them. They were like a little window into ideas and feelings. **Did you know?** The very first cartoons were sometimes drawn on walls or old paper a very, very long time ago! People have always loved to tell stories with pictures!

A Very, Very Long Time Away

Now, here’s the part that might make you a little sad for a moment, but remember, it has a happy ending! A long time ago, Biniam was taken to a place called a prison. A prison is a place where grown-ups go if they break big rules or laws. But here’s the really strange thing: Biniam was never told what rule he broke! No one ever said what he did wrong. He was just taken away. Can you imagine being somewhere for a long, long time, and no one tells you why? It’s like being sent to your room, but your mum or dad never tells you what you did naughty. That’s what happened to Biniam. He stayed in that place for FIFTEEN years! That is a super, super long time. Let’s think about how long 15 years is. If you are 7 years old, 15 years is more than twice your whole life! It’s like waiting for your birthday party to come around fifteen times. It’s a very, very, very long wait. For all that time, Biniam was away from his family, his friends, and his drawing table.

What Does a Cartoonist Do? (A little more!)

Let’s talk a little more about what Biniam did. He drew cartoons for newspapers. Think of newspapers like big sheets of paper that tell you the news of the day. But Biniam didn’t just write words; he drew pictures. His pictures were like little jokes or clever messages. They helped people understand the world in a fun way. Sometimes, when things are not fair or good in the world, cartoonists like Biniam draw pictures to show it. They might draw a picture that makes you think, "Hmm, maybe that's not right." This is a way for artists to share their ideas and help people see things differently. It’s a special kind of talking, but with a pen and paper! **Did you know?** Some cartoon characters you see on TV, like Mickey Mouse or SpongeBob, started as simple drawings before they became famous!

A Country Called Eritrea

Eritrea is the country where Biniam lives. It’s a beautiful country in East Africa, right next to the Red Sea. Imagine sunny beaches and old cities! The capital city of Eritrea is called Asmara. It has very special buildings that are really old and pretty. People in Eritrea speak different languages, and they have wonderful food and music. It’s important to remember that every country, even beautiful ones, can sometimes have problems. In some places, it’s not easy for people to say what they really think or to share all their ideas, especially if those ideas are a little different from what the leaders want. This is what happened around the time Biniam was taken away. Newspapers and TV stations could not always say what they wanted freely. **Did you know?** Eritrea is home to some amazing animals like gazelles and ostriches! It also has a very long coastline along the Red Sea, which is full of beautiful fish!

Hooray for Freedom!

But now, the super happy news! After 15 years, Biniam Solomon has been set free! Can you imagine how excited his family must be? His mum, dad, brothers, and sisters (if he has them) are probably jumping up and down with joy! He gets to go home. He gets to see the sky freely, eat his favourite food, and talk to anyone he wants. Being free means you can walk where you want, say what you think (nicely, of course!), and draw all the pictures you dream of. We hope Biniam is now sitting somewhere very comfy, maybe with a cup of tea, and starting to draw again. Maybe he will draw a picture about how happy he is to be home! This is a big, happy moment for Biniam and for many people who care about artists and their freedom. It shows that even after a very long wait, good things can happen.

Why Are Drawings So Important?

You might wonder why drawings or words are so important that someone could be put away for them. Well, dear friends, it’s because sharing ideas is a very big part of being human. When people can talk, write, or draw about what they think, it helps everyone learn and grow. It helps us understand each other better. Imagine if you had a super clever idea for a game, but no one let you tell anyone about it. That wouldn’t be fair, right? It’s the same with grown-up ideas, even if they are about serious things. We all learn when we hear different ideas, even if we don't always agree with them. It helps us make our world a better, kinder, and smarter place. **Did you know?** In many countries, there is something called "freedom of speech." This means people have the right to say what they think, even if others don't agree. It's a very important right!

Let’s Hope for More Smiles!

So, as we wave goodbye to this news story, let's send a big, warm wish to Biniam Solomon. We hope he is happy and healthy, and that he gets to draw many, many more wonderful cartoons. We also hope that in every country, artists, writers, and everyone else can always share their ideas freely and safely. This story reminds us that every person's voice and every person's drawing is important. Let’s all try to be kind, listen to each other, and celebrate the freedom to share our wonderful ideas, just like Biniam Solomon. Hooray for Biniam!
summary: Biniam Solomon, an artist who draws funny and clever pictures, has finally been set free in Eritrea after being in a special kind of place for 15 very long years without anyone telling him why he was there.
whyMatters: This is important because it teaches us that everyone should be able to share their ideas through drawings or words, and it's not fair to be put away for such a long time without knowing what you did wrong.

originalTitle: Death toll from Ethiopia floods and landslides rises to more than 100
title: Big Rains and Moving Earth in Ethiopia!
content:
Hello, amazing friends all over Africa and beyond! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to tell you about something important that happened in a beautiful country called Ethiopia. Ethiopia is a big, lovely country in East Africa, full of amazing people, animals, and yummy food! Sometimes, nature can be very powerful. Recently, Ethiopia had some very, very strong rain. So much rain fell that it caused big problems called floods and landslides. It's a bit like when you fill a bathtub too much, and the water spills over – but on a much, much bigger scale!

What Happened in Ethiopia?

Imagine the sky pouring down water, not just for a little while, but for a very long time! In Ethiopia, this big rain kept falling and falling. All that water made the rivers swell up and burst their banks. When this happens, water goes everywhere it's not supposed to be, like into people's homes and over roads. This is a flood. And when the ground gets super wet, especially on hills and mountains, it can become very soft and squishy. Sometimes, the wet earth and rocks can't hold on anymore and they start to slide down the hill. This is called a landslide. It’s like a giant mud slide! Sadly, because of these floods and landslides, more than 100 people sadly lost their lives. This means some families lost someone they loved very much. Many homes were also broken or washed away, and people had to leave their houses to find a safe place. It’s a very sad time for them, and our hearts go out to all the families in Ethiopia. Did you know? Ethiopia is home to some of the world's oldest human fossils, meaning people have lived there for a very, very long time!

Why Was the Rain So Strong?

Our planet Earth is a wonderful home, but sometimes it gets a little bit out of balance. Scientists, who are very smart people who study our world, say that storms are becoming much, much stronger than they used to be. It means when it rains, it really, really rains! It's not just a gentle shower, but sometimes like a giant waterfall from the sky. Did you know? Rain is part of Earth's water cycle. Water goes up into the sky as vapor, makes clouds, and then falls back down as rain, snow, or hail!

What are Floods?

Think about a big puddle. Now, imagine that puddle growing so big that it covers your street, your yard, and even goes into your house! That's what a flood is. Too much water in places where it shouldn't be. Floods can make it hard to get around, damage buildings, and even be dangerous if the water is moving very fast. When rivers overflow, they can cover huge areas of land. This makes it difficult for people to travel, for children to go to school, and for farmers to tend to their crops. People often need help to get to a safe place and to get food and clean water. Did you know? The longest river in the world, the Nile River, has some of its sources in Ethiopia!

What are Landslides?

Imagine building a sandcastle on a slope at the beach. If you pour lots of water on it, what happens? The sand starts to slide down! A landslide is similar, but with real earth, rocks, and mud on hills or mountains. When the ground gets super, super wet from heavy rain, it can’t hold itself up anymore and just slides down. This can be very fast and dangerous, sweeping away everything in its path, like trees and houses. Landslides can block roads and even change the shape of the land. They are a powerful reminder of how strong nature can be, especially when the ground is really wet and there are steep hills. Did you know? Mountains are formed over millions of years when big parts of Earth's crust push against each other!

Helping Hands

When bad things like floods and landslides happen, people always come together to help. In Ethiopia, brave rescue workers, kind neighbours, and helpful people from all over are working hard to find people, give them food, clean water, and a safe place to stay. It's so important that we all look out for each other, especially when times are tough. Many organizations and people are sending aid, which means sending things like blankets, medicines, and food to help the affected families. Even though it's sad, it's also wonderful to see how people care and help each other. Did you know? Sharing and helping others makes everyone feel better, not just the person receiving help!

Our Earth is Getting Warm

Now, for the really important part that scientists are telling us. Our planet Earth is getting warmer. Think of it like Earth having a little bit of a fever. This warming is called 'global warming' or 'climate change'. It's happening because of things people do, like using too much energy from burning fuels that make smoke go into the air. When the Earth gets warmer, it changes the weather all around the world. It can make some places hotter and drier, and other places get super heavy rain, like what happened in Ethiopia. A warmer Earth means more energy in the air, and that energy can make storms much more powerful. Did you know? The Sun is a giant star that gives Earth all its light and warmth, but Earth also makes some of its own warmth!

Why Does a Warm Earth Mean More Rain?

It might sound a bit strange, but a warmer Earth means more water can evaporate from the oceans and land into the air. When there's more water vapor in the air, the clouds get really full, and when they finally burst, they let out a massive amount of rain. It's like a bigger sponge soaking up more water and then squeezing it out all at once! This is why scientists say that big, strong storms are becoming more common. So, the heavy rains in Ethiopia, which caused the floods and landslides, are partly because our planet is getting warmer. We need to take care of our Earth to keep it healthy and balanced. Did you know? Clouds are made of tiny, tiny drops of water or ice crystals floating in the sky!

What Can We Do?

Even as kids, you can help! You can learn more about our planet and how to keep it healthy. You can save electricity by turning off lights when you leave a room. You can help plant trees, because trees are amazing at soaking up water and keeping the soil strong. You can also tell your parents and friends about global warming and why it's important to take care of our Earth. Every little bit helps to make our world a safer and happier place for everyone. Let's remember the people in Ethiopia and hope they can rebuild their homes and lives soon. We send them our strength and love from all over Africa and beyond! Did you know? Trees are like Earth's superheroes! They clean the air, give us shade, and help prevent floods by soaking up water with their roots!
summary: Heavy rain in Ethiopia caused big floods and landslides, sadly leading to many people losing their lives and homes, and scientists say these stronger storms are happening because our Earth is getting warmer.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how a warmer Earth can make big storms, hurting people and places, and it reminds us to take care of our planet so everyone can be safe.
